response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, architectural thinking, and testing discipline, the role requires a senior C++ specialist and the resume shows only 6 months of documented C++ experience at National Instruments (2014-2015). The primary career focus has been web and full-stack development using TypeScript and Node.js, with no industrial automation, IoT, or embedded systems background. The absence of security standards expertise (IEC 62443) and industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA) further compounds the misalignment. The recent 7-month career gap is also a concern.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship with proven TDD culture and testing expertise (created fluent-asserts library and Trial test framework)
Architectural design and systems thinking demonstrated through event-driven GISCollective platform and high-performance microservices (Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second)
Proven mentoring capability through workshops, code kata sessions, and open-source community leadership
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015); primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js web development, not systems or embedded programming
No industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or manufacturing background; all experience is in web platforms, GIS applications, and data services
Lacks industrial cybersecurity knowledge (SDL, IEC 62443 standards) and experience with required protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA, DNP 3.0)

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or embedded systems background
